articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Corridor' Project, Pledging Billions for Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'National Green Hydrogen Corridor' project, a multi-billion dollar initiative aimed at establishing dedicated infrastructure for green hydrogen production, storage, and transportation across key industrial clusters.
• The project targets the creation of at least three major Green Hydrogen Production Hubs by 2030, integrating renewable energy sources directly with electrolysis plants.
• It includes provisions for incentives and subsidies for both hydrogen producers and consumers, focusing on hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and heavy-duty transport.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's net-zero targets by 2070 and enhance energy security by reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports.
• Skill development programs and R&D support for advanced electrolysis technologies are integral components of the corridor's strategic framework.

articles.headline: Monsoon Session Concludes: Key Bills on Digital Data Protection and Social Security Passed
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament's Monsoon Session concluded today, marking the passage of several crucial legislations, most notably the Digital Data Protection Bill and amendments to the Social Security Code.
• The Digital Data Protection Bill aims to safeguard personal data, establish a robust framework for data fiduciaries, and grant significant rights to data principals, with provisions for a Data Protection Board.
• Amendments to the Social Security Code focus on expanding coverage for gig and platform workers, streamlining compliance, and enhancing benefits for vulnerable sections of society.
• The session also saw intense debates on economic recovery, inflation, and federal fiscal relations, with the opposition raising concerns over various policy implementations.
• A bill proposing reforms in the cooperative sector was also introduced, aiming to strengthen governance and financial management of cooperative societies.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level, signaling confidence in inflation moderation.
• The MPC noted a gradual easing of headline inflation, driven by favorable base effects and supply-side measures, though it highlighted potential risks from volatile food prices and global crude oil movements.
• Projections for India's GDP growth for the current fiscal year were reaffirmed, with cautious optimism regarding sustained domestic demand and investment uptick.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while keeping inflation within the target band of 2-6%, emphasizing liquidity management tools.
• Experts suggest the decision reflects a wait-and-watch approach, anticipating further data to confirm a durable disinflationary trend before considering any rate adjustments.

articles.headline: India-Japan 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue between India and Japan concluded in Tokyo, with Defence and Foreign Ministers from both sides reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaboration in emerging security domains like cyber and space.
• Economic partnership was a key agenda, with talks on boosting trade, investment, and supply chain resilience, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors.
• Both nations expressed concerns over regional security challenges and reiterated their resolve to work together through multilateral forums like Quad and G20.
• The dialogue underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and Japan, positioning them as key pillars of stability and growth in Asia.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Electoral Bonds Scheme on October 1st
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced that a specially constituted bench will begin hearing a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme on October 1st, signaling a crucial legal review.
• The petitions argue that the scheme lacks transparency, infringes upon the 'right to know' of citizens, and promotes opaque political funding, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Central Government has consistently defended the scheme, asserting it aims to curb black money in elections and ensure donor anonymity, thereby protecting contributors from political victimization.
• This review comes after previous inconclusive hearings and growing public demand for greater accountability in political financing.
• The outcome could significantly reshape the landscape of political funding in India, with implications for electoral transparency and democratic processes.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission: Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar samples back to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission will involve a complex multi-module architecture, including an orbiter, lander, ascender, and re-entry module.
• Scientists are focusing on identifying potential landing sites in the lunar south polar region, known for its water ice deposits, which could yield valuable scientific data.
• The mission aims to demonstrate advanced capabilities in precision landing, robotic sample collection, and safe return of extraterrestrial material, marking a significant technological leap for ISRO.
• International collaborations are also being explored for data sharing and mission support, enhancing India's standing in global space exploration.

articles.headline: NEP Implementation: Government to Launch 'Digital Universities' Pilot Program
articles.explainer:
• As part of the ongoing National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, the Ministry of Education announced a pilot program for 'Digital Universities' to expand access to quality higher education.
• These digital universities will offer a wide range of courses and degrees entirely online, leveraging advanced e-learning platforms, AI-powered personalized learning, and virtual labs.
• The initiative aims to address the challenges of geographical barriers, faculty shortages, and limited infrastructure in traditional higher education institutions.
• Special emphasis will be placed on skill-based and interdisciplinary programs, aligning with the NEP's focus on holistic and flexible learning pathways.
• A regulatory framework for digital universities is also being developed to ensure quality assurance, accreditation, and equivalency of degrees.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Discuss Roadmap for Climate Finance Mobilization Ahead of Leaders' Summit
articles.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors convened virtually to finalize a roadmap for mobilizing climate finance, ahead of the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Key discussions revolved around scaling up funding for climate mitigation and adaptation in developing countries, with emphasis on multilateral development banks (MDBs) reforms and innovative financing mechanisms.
• India, as a significant voice for the Global South, pushed for developed nations to fulfill their $100 billion climate finance commitment and advocated for equitable burden-sharing.
• The roadmap includes proposals for blended finance, carbon markets, and private sector engagement to bridge the substantial climate finance gap.
• The meeting highlighted the urgency of addressing climate change impacts while ensuring sustainable economic growth, particularly for vulnerable economies.

articles.headline: Ministry of Urban Affairs Launches 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' for Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Urban Affairs launched the 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission,' a new initiative to enhance preparedness and response to urban flooding in ten major Indian cities prone to heavy rainfall.
• The mission focuses on integrated urban water management, including improved drainage systems, rainwater harvesting, rejuvenation of urban wetlands, and early warning systems.
• It emphasizes the adoption of nature-based solutions and climate-resilient infrastructure development to mitigate flood risks and minimize economic losses.
• Capacity building for municipal bodies, urban planners, and disaster response teams is a core component, along with community engagement for effective implementation.
• Funding will be provided through a combination of central assistance, state contributions, and leveraging private sector investment in flood management technologies.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Traction; Several Nations Express Interest
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering model of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is gaining significant international recognition, with several developing nations expressing interest in adopting similar frameworks.
• A recent UN report highlighted India's DPI as a blueprint for accelerating digital transformation and financial inclusion, especially for populations previously excluded from formal services.
• The open-source nature of many Indian DPI components allows for flexible adaptation, making it attractive to countries seeking cost-effective and scalable digital solutions.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral discussions to share its expertise and support the deployment of DPI in partner countries, particularly in Africa and Southeast Asia.
• This global outreach not only strengthens India's soft power but also opens avenues for technology exports and partnerships in the digital economy.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah: Ministry Reports Successful Adaptation, Expansion Plans Underway
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released an update on Project Cheetah, reporting successful adaptation and breeding outcomes for the translocated cheetahs in Kuno National Park.
• The report highlighted improved survival rates of cubs and adult cheetahs, attributing success to diligent monitoring, prey base management, and anti-poaching measures.
• Based on positive results, the Ministry announced plans to identify additional suitable sites for cheetah reintroduction to establish a meta-population across India.
• This expansion is crucial for ensuring genetic diversity and long-term viability of the cheetah population, marking a significant step in India's biodiversity conservation efforts.
• Community engagement and eco-tourism development around cheetah habitats are also being prioritized to foster local support for conservation.

articles.headline: Government Initiates Public Consultation for National AI Governance Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a nationwide public consultation process for developing India's comprehensive National AI Governance Framework.
• The framework aims to promote responsible AI development and deployment, ensuring ethical considerations, data privacy, accountability, and fairness in AI applications.
• Key areas of discussion include AI safety, intellectual property rights in AI-generated content, regulatory sandboxes for innovation, and skill development for an AI-ready workforce.
• The consultation seeks inputs from industry stakeholders, academia, civil society organizations, and the general public to foster an inclusive and robust policy.
• This move reflects India's proactive stance in shaping the future of AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being and regulatory oversight.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Unveils Updated Maritime Security Strategy Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy unveiled its updated Maritime Security Strategy, outlining a comprehensive approach to safeguard India's extensive coastline, island territories, and maritime interests in the Indo-Pacific.
• The strategy emphasizes enhanced surveillance capabilities, technological upgrades, and greater interoperability with friendly navies to counter traditional and non-traditional threats.
• Focus areas include anti-piracy operations, freedom of navigation, protection of sea lines of communication (SLOCs), and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief (HADR) missions.
• It also addresses challenges posed by increasing naval presence of external powers in the Indian Ocean Region and the growing threat of maritime terrorism and illicit trafficking.
• The strategy aligns with India's vision for SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region), promoting regional cooperation and a rules-based maritime order.

articles.headline: Government Fast-Tracks Critical Minerals Exploration and Auction Policy to Boost Domestic Supply
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines announced accelerated implementation of its Critical Minerals Exploration and Auction Policy, aiming to significantly boost domestic supply chains for key industrial and strategic sectors.
• The policy focuses on identifying and extracting minerals essential for renewable energy technologies, electric vehicles, defense, and electronics, such as Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements.
• Incentives for private sector participation in exploration and mining, streamlined regulatory clearances, and adoption of advanced exploration technologies are key features.
• This move is critical for India's Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative, reducing import dependence and enhancing national security in the face of global supply chain vulnerabilities.
• Environmental safeguards and sustainable mining practices are integral to the policy, ensuring responsible resource extraction.

articles.headline: India Proposes 'Global South Health Initiative' at UN General Assembly Preparatory Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India, during a preparatory meeting for the upcoming UN General Assembly, proposed a 'Global South Health Initiative' aimed at fostering collaborative healthcare solutions and equitable access to medical resources among developing nations.
• The initiative focuses on knowledge sharing in vaccine development, affordable drug manufacturing, digital health infrastructure, and training of healthcare professionals.
• It seeks to leverage India's experience as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its advancements in digital public goods for health.
• The proposal emphasizes a collective approach to address health disparities, pandemic preparedness, and achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) in the Global South.
• This move reinforces India's leadership in advocating for the interests and needs of developing countries on the global stage.

articles.headline: India Crosses 150 GW Renewable Energy Capacity Milestone, Solar Leads the Way
articles.explainer:
• India announced a significant achievement, crossing 150 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, with solar power contributing the largest share to this milestone.
• This growth is attributed to favorable government policies, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes for solar manufacturing and simplified rooftop solar installation norms.
• The increased capacity significantly boosts India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The push for renewable energy is also enhancing energy access in remote areas and reducing the country's carbon footprint.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition, but the overall trajectory indicates a robust transition towards a green energy future.

articles.headline: E-Waste Management Rules 2025: Government Notifies Amendments for Extended Producer Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, now termed 'E-Waste Management Rules 2025,' with enhanced provisions for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• The revised rules mandate stricter collection targets for electronic waste, broaden the scope of covered electronic products, and introduce a credit-based system for EPR compliance.
• Emphasis is placed on promoting circular economy principles, encouraging repair, reuse, and recycling of electronic goods to minimize environmental impact.
• Penalties for non-compliance have been stiffened, and a centralized portal for EPR registration and reporting has been introduced to improve transparency and enforcement.
• The amendments aim to tackle India's rapidly growing e-waste challenge, which poses significant health and environmental hazards if not managed properly.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Technical Discussions on Indus Waters Treaty, Focus on Hydropower Projects
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in Vienna, facilitated by the World Bank, focusing on ongoing hydropower projects.
• The talks aimed to address Pakistan's concerns regarding the design and operational aspects of certain Indian hydropower projects on the Western rivers, particularly the Kishanganga and Ratle projects.
• India reiterated its stance that its projects fully comply with the provisions of the IWT, which governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
• The dialogue is part of the continuous mechanism under the IWT to resolve disputes amicably and ensure the treaty's smooth implementation.
• Despite broader bilateral tensions, the IWT has largely endured as a successful example of water-sharing cooperation between the two nations.

articles.headline: National Cyber Security Strategy: Government Prioritizes Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator's office announced an accelerated implementation of the National Cyber Security Strategy, with a heightened focus on protecting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The updated strategy includes enhanced measures for threat intelligence sharing, incident response protocols, and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals across public and private sectors.
• Special attention is being given to securing sectors like energy, finance, telecom, and transportation, which are vital for national functioning and economic stability.
• Collaboration with international partners and private sector entities is a key pillar to counter sophisticated cyber threats and develop resilient cyber defenses.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard India's digital economy and protect citizens' data from evolving cyber warfare and criminal activities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's 'National Green Hydrogen Corridor' project, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to establish at least three major Green Hydrogen Production Hubs by 2030.
2. It includes provisions for incentives for hydrogen producers but not for consumers.
3. It primarily focuses on integrating renewable energy sources directly with electrolysis plants.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The project targets the creation of at least three major Green Hydrogen Production Hubs by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: The initiative includes provisions for incentives and subsidies for both hydrogen producers and consumers. Statement 3 is correct: It focuses on integrating renewable energy sources directly with electrolysis plants.

prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct as the Digital Data Protection Bill aims to safeguard personal data and establish a Data Protection Board. Statement 3 refers to amendments to the Social Security Code, not the Digital Data Protection Bill.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches New Scheme to Boost Heritage Tourism and Local Economies
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled a new 'Heritage Tourism Development Scheme' aimed at promoting lesser-known historical sites, cultural landscapes, and intangible heritage across India.
• The scheme focuses on integrated development of heritage circuits, including improved connectivity, visitor amenities, digital interpretation centers, and local craft promotion.
• It seeks to create sustainable livelihoods for local communities by involving them in heritage management, guiding services, and cultural performances.
• Special emphasis will be placed on leveraging technology for virtual reality experiences and augmented reality tours to attract a wider audience, including international tourists.
• The initiative aligns with the 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign, encouraging domestic tourism and showcasing India's rich cultural diversity.

GS2.headline: Monsoon Session Concludes: Key Bills on Digital Data Protection and Social Security Passed
GS2.explainer:
• The Parliament's Monsoon Session concluded today, marking the passage of several crucial legislations, most notably the Digital Data Protection Bill and amendments to the Social Security Code.
• The Digital Data Protection Bill aims to safeguard personal data, establish a robust framework for data fiduciaries, and grant significant rights to data principals, with provisions for a Data Protection Board.
• Amendments to the Social Security Code focus on expanding coverage for gig and platform workers, streamlining compliance, and enhancing benefits for vulnerable sections of society.
• The session also saw intense debates on economic recovery, inflation, and federal fiscal relations, with the opposition raising concerns over various policy implementations.
• A bill proposing reforms in the cooperative sector was also introduced, aiming to strengthen governance and financial management of cooperative societies.

GS2.headline: India-Japan 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue between India and Japan concluded in Tokyo, with Defence and Foreign Ministers from both sides reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaboration in emerging security domains like cyber and space.
• Economic partnership was a key agenda, with talks on boosting trade, investment, and supply chain resilience, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors.
• Both nations expressed concerns over regional security challenges and reiterated their resolve to work together through multilateral forums like Quad and G20.
• The dialogue underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and Japan, positioning them as key pillars of stability and growth in Asia.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Electoral Bonds Scheme on October 1st
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced that a specially constituted bench will begin hearing a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme on October 1st, signaling a crucial legal review.
• The petitions argue that the scheme lacks transparency, infringes upon the 'right to know' of citizens, and promotes opaque political funding, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Central Government has consistently defended the scheme, asserting it aims to curb black money in elections and ensure donor anonymity, thereby protecting contributors from political victimization.
• This review comes after previous inconclusive hearings and growing public demand for greater accountability in political financing.
• The outcome could significantly reshape the landscape of political funding in India, with implications for electoral transparency and democratic processes.

GS2.headline: NEP Implementation: Government to Launch 'Digital Universities' Pilot Program
GS2.explainer:
• As part of the ongoing National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, the Ministry of Education announced a pilot program for 'Digital Universities' to expand access to quality higher education.
• These digital universities will offer a wide range of courses and degrees entirely online, leveraging advanced e-learning platforms, AI-powered personalized learning, and virtual labs.
• The initiative aims to address the challenges of geographical barriers, faculty shortages, and limited infrastructure in traditional higher education institutions.
• Special emphasis will be placed on skill-based and interdisciplinary programs, aligning with the NEP's focus on holistic and flexible learning pathways.
• A regulatory framework for digital universities is also being developed to ensure quality assurance, accreditation, and equivalency of degrees.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Discuss Roadmap for Climate Finance Mobilization Ahead of Leaders' Summit
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors convened virtually to finalize a roadmap for mobilizing climate finance, ahead of the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Key discussions revolved around scaling up funding for climate mitigation and adaptation in developing countries, with emphasis on multilateral development banks (MDBs) reforms and innovative financing mechanisms.
• India, as a significant voice for the Global South, pushed for developed nations to fulfill their $100 billion climate finance commitment and advocated for equitable burden-sharing.
• The roadmap includes proposals for blended finance, carbon markets, and private sector engagement to bridge the substantial climate finance gap.
• The meeting highlighted the urgency of addressing climate change impacts while ensuring sustainable economic growth, particularly for vulnerable economies.

• It seeks to leverage India's experience as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its advancements in digital public goods for health.
• The proposal emphasizes a collective approach to address health disparities, pandemic preparedness, and achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) in the Global South.
• This move reinforces India's leadership in advocating for the interests and needs of developing countries on the global stage.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Corridor' Project, Pledging Billions for Energy Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'National Green Hydrogen Corridor' project, a multi-billion dollar initiative aimed at establishing dedicated infrastructure for green hydrogen production, storage, and transportation across key industrial clusters.
• The project targets the creation of at least three major Green Hydrogen Production Hubs by 2030, integrating renewable energy sources directly with electrolysis plants.
• It includes provisions for incentives and subsidies for both hydrogen producers and consumers, focusing on hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and heavy-duty transport.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's net-zero targets by 2070 and enhance energy security by reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports.
• Skill development programs and R&D support for advanced electrolysis technologies are integral components of the corridor's strategic framework.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level, signaling confidence in inflation moderation.
• The MPC noted a gradual easing of headline inflation, driven by favorable base effects and supply-side measures, though it highlighted potential risks from volatile food prices and global crude oil movements.
• Projections for India's GDP growth for the current fiscal year were reaffirmed, with cautious optimism regarding sustained domestic demand and investment uptick.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while keeping inflation within the target band of 2-6%, emphasizing liquidity management tools.
• Experts suggest the decision reflects a wait-and-watch approach, anticipating further data to confirm a durable disinflationary trend before considering any rate adjustments.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission: Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar samples back to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission will involve a complex multi-module architecture, including an orbiter, lander, ascender, and re-entry module.
• Scientists are focusing on identifying potential landing sites in the lunar south polar region, known for its water ice deposits, which could yield valuable scientific data.
• The mission aims to demonstrate advanced capabilities in precision landing, robotic sample collection, and safe return of extraterrestrial material, marking a significant technological leap for ISRO.
• International collaborations are also being explored for data sharing and mission support, enhancing India's standing in global space exploration.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Urban Affairs Launches 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' for Major Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Urban Affairs launched the 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission,' a new initiative to enhance preparedness and response to urban flooding in ten major Indian cities prone to heavy rainfall.
• The mission focuses on integrated urban water management, including improved drainage systems, rainwater harvesting, rejuvenation of urban wetlands, and early warning systems.
• It emphasizes the adoption of nature-based solutions and climate-resilient infrastructure development to mitigate flood risks and minimize economic losses.
• Capacity building for municipal bodies, urban planners, and disaster response teams is a core component, along with community engagement for effective implementation.
• Funding will be provided through a combination of central assistance, state contributions, and leveraging private sector investment in flood management technologies.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Traction; Several Nations Express Interest
GS3.explainer:
• India's pioneering model of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is gaining significant international recognition, with several developing nations expressing interest in adopting similar frameworks.
• A recent UN report highlighted India's DPI as a blueprint for accelerating digital transformation and financial inclusion, especially for populations previously excluded from formal services.
• The open-source nature of many Indian DPI components allows for flexible adaptation, making it attractive to countries seeking cost-effective and scalable digital solutions.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral discussions to share its expertise and support the deployment of DPI in partner countries, particularly in Africa and Southeast Asia.
• This global outreach not only strengthens India's soft power but also opens avenues for technology exports and partnerships in the digital economy.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah: Ministry Reports Successful Adaptation, Expansion Plans Underway
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released an update on Project Cheetah, reporting successful adaptation and breeding outcomes for the translocated cheetahs in Kuno National Park.
• The report highlighted improved survival rates of cubs and adult cheetahs, attributing success to diligent monitoring, prey base management, and anti-poaching measures.
• Based on positive results, the Ministry announced plans to identify additional suitable sites for cheetah reintroduction to establish a meta-population across India.
• This expansion is crucial for ensuring genetic diversity and long-term viability of the cheetah population, marking a significant step in India's biodiversity conservation efforts.
• Community engagement and eco-tourism development around cheetah habitats are also being prioritized to foster local support for conservation.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates Public Consultation for National AI Governance Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a nationwide public consultation process for developing India's comprehensive National AI Governance Framework.
• The framework aims to promote responsible AI development and deployment, ensuring ethical considerations, data privacy, accountability, and fairness in AI applications.
• Key areas of discussion include AI safety, intellectual property rights in AI-generated content, regulatory sandboxes for innovation, and skill development for an AI-ready workforce.
• The consultation seeks inputs from industry stakeholders, academia, civil society organizations, and the general public to foster an inclusive and robust policy.
• This move reflects India's proactive stance in shaping the future of AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being and regulatory oversight.

GS3.headline: Indian Navy Unveils Updated Maritime Security Strategy Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy unveiled its updated Maritime Security Strategy, outlining a comprehensive approach to safeguard India's extensive coastline, island territories, and maritime interests in the Indo-Pacific.
• The strategy emphasizes enhanced surveillance capabilities, technological upgrades, and greater interoperability with friendly navies to counter traditional and non-traditional threats.
• Focus areas include anti-piracy operations, freedom of navigation, protection of sea lines of communication (SLOCs), and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief (HADR) missions.
• It also addresses challenges posed by increasing naval presence of external powers in the Indian Ocean Region and the growing threat of maritime terrorism and illicit trafficking.
• The strategy aligns with India's vision for SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region), promoting regional cooperation and a rules-based maritime order.

GS3.headline: Government Fast-Tracks Critical Minerals Exploration and Auction Policy to Boost Domestic Supply
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines announced accelerated implementation of its Critical Minerals Exploration and Auction Policy, aiming to significantly boost domestic supply chains for key industrial and strategic sectors.
• The policy focuses on identifying and extracting minerals essential for renewable energy technologies, electric vehicles, defense, and electronics, such as Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements.
• Incentives for private sector participation in exploration and mining, streamlined regulatory clearances, and adoption of advanced exploration technologies are key features.
• This move is critical for India's Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative, reducing import dependence and enhancing national security in the face of global supply chain vulnerabilities.
• Environmental safeguards and sustainable mining practices are integral to the policy, ensuring responsible resource extraction.

GS3.headline: India Crosses 150 GW Renewable Energy Capacity Milestone, Solar Leads the Way
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a significant achievement, crossing 150 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, with solar power contributing the largest share to this milestone.
• This growth is attributed to favorable government policies, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes for solar manufacturing and simplified rooftop solar installation norms.
• The increased capacity significantly boosts India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The push for renewable energy is also enhancing energy access in remote areas and reducing the country's carbon footprint.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition, but the overall trajectory indicates a robust transition towards a green energy future.

GS3.headline: E-Waste Management Rules 2025: Government Notifies Amendments for Extended Producer Responsibility
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, now termed 'E-Waste Management Rules 2025,' with enhanced provisions for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• The revised rules mandate stricter collection targets for electronic waste, broaden the scope of covered electronic products, and introduce a credit-based system for EPR compliance.
• Emphasis is placed on promoting circular economy principles, encouraging repair, reuse, and recycling of electronic goods to minimize environmental impact.
• Penalties for non-compliance have been stiffened, and a centralized portal for EPR registration and reporting has been introduced to improve transparency and enforcement.
• The amendments aim to tackle India's rapidly growing e-waste challenge, which poses significant health and environmental hazards if not managed properly.

GS3.headline: National Cyber Security Strategy: Government Prioritizes Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator's office announced an accelerated implementation of the National Cyber Security Strategy, with a heightened focus on protecting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The updated strategy includes enhanced measures for threat intelligence sharing, incident response protocols, and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals across public and private sectors.
• Special attention is being given to securing sectors like energy, finance, telecom, and transportation, which are vital for national functioning and economic stability.
• Collaboration with international partners and private sector entities is a key pillar to counter sophisticated cyber threats and develop resilient cyber defenses.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard India's digital economy and protect citizens' data from evolving cyber warfare and criminal activities.
